differential_diagnoses: Dabrafenib and trametinib-induced systemic drug reaction (drug-induced fever/hepatotoxicity)
Anaplasmosis (given patient geography and clinical presentation)
Hemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis (HLH) secondary to drug therapy or malignancy
Systemic inflammatory response syndrome due to occult biliary pathology
Autoimmune hepatitis or immune-related adverse event (irAE)
next_steps: Perform peripheral blood smear for morulae to rule out anaplasmosis
Order serum ferritin, triglycerides, soluble CD25, and fibrinogen levels to evaluate for HLH
Consult hematology/oncology regarding permanent discontinuation of BRAF/MEK inhibitors
Obtain liver biopsy for histopathologic evaluation of hepatitis
Perform echocardiogram to rule out endocarditis
Order imaging with IV contrast (e.g., MRI/MRCP) to better evaluate hepatic pathology and biliary tract

differential_diagnoses: Dabrafenib and trametinib-induced systemic drug reaction (including granulomatous hepatitis)
Hemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis (HLH) secondary to drug therapy or malignancy
Metastatic melanoma with rapid hepatic progression
Autoimmune hepatitis or immune-related adverse event (irAE)
Systemic Inflammatory Response Syndrome (SIRS) secondary to occult biliary pathology
next_steps: Perform peripheral blood smear to evaluate for morulae and intraerythrocytic parasites (Babesia)
Order serum ferritin, triglycerides, soluble CD25, and fibrinogen to evaluate for HLH
Obtain liver biopsy for histopathologic evaluation (to rule out granulomatous hepatitis or metastatic disease)
Perform MRI/MRCP with contrast to evaluate hepatic parenchyma and biliary anatomy
Permanently discontinue dabrafenib and trametinib therapy
Obtain LDH, haptoglobin, and reticulocyte count to evaluate for hemolysis
